notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

notes.content: APPLIED PHYSICS, COMPUTATIONAL PHYSICS CONCENTRATION B.S.

Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: PHYS 5A – Introduction to Physics I* PHYS 5B – Introduction to Physics II* PHYS 5C – Introduction to Physics III* MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 23A – Vector Calculus *PHYS 5A, PHYS 5B, and PHYS 5C must be completed with a cumulative GPA of 2.70 or higher. Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements. Winter Applicants Screening Policy Students applying for winter quarter admission are required to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, in addition to those required for fall transfer: PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV MATH 23B – Vector Calculus See the "Recommended Preparation" and "Additional Major Preparation Courses" sections below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV and CSE 20 – Beginning Programming in Python , or their articulated equivalents, to ensure timely graduation. Students who do not complete these preparatory course before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Please visit the Physics Department website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Applied Physics B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall:


  1. PHYS 5A – Introduction to Physics I*
  2. PHYS 5B – Introduction to Physics II*
  3. PHYS 5C – Introduction to Physics III*
  4. MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
  5. MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
  6. MATH 23A – Vector Calculus


*PHYS 5A, PHYS 5B, and PHYS 5C must be completed with a cumulative GPA of 2.70 or higher.


Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements.


Winter Applicants Screening Policy

Students applying for winter quarter admission are required to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, in addition to those required for fall transfer:


  1. PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV
  2. MATH 23B – Vector Calculus


See the "Recommended Preparation" and "Additional Major Preparation Courses" sections below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV and CSE 20 – Beginning Programming in Python, or their articulated equivalents, to ensure timely graduation. Students who do not complete these preparatory course before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.



Additional Information for Transfer

Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Please visit the Physics Department website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Applied Physics B.S. Program Statement
